Mom and dad could not afford the nursing home bills along with their home mortgage & utilities. Nancie and her partners helped us find funding for the nursing home and keep mom in their home. Shortly after mom was diagnosed with stage 4 breast cancer. We were able to take care of her at her home and give her an additional 3.5 years to spend with her family. If not for Nancie, her firm and partners we could not have spent this special time with our parents and find the joy out of an incredibly hard situation. We are incredibly thankful and grateful for helping through this decade; couldn’t have done so much for them without you!

Megan was polite when I finally spoke to her, but clearly rushed. She told me to be at court at 8am and wait for her. We never established a clear plan on where or how we would meet. She also instructed me to leave my phone in the car.

I waited outside the courtroom for over 10 minutes, asking multiple attorneys if they were Megan. As my anxiety grew, I eventually went inside and waited another 50 minutes. Megan never showed.

When I asked another attorney for help, they checked with the prosecutor and told me I had been marked as a no-show.

I left the courthouse frustrated and called the firm. Aaron answered and immediately demanded payment for a future court date.

This was a stressful and completely avoidable experience. Poor communication and lack of accountability. I would not recommend this firm.

I want to share my experience working with attorney Terri McAdams, because her help truly changed my life. My driver’s license was revoked back in 2015, and over the years I was arrested five different times for driving on a revoked license. After that, I stopped driving completely and relied on Ubers for everything—getting to work, going home, even running simple errands. It became extremely expensive and stressful, and I knew I couldn’t keep living that way. I needed help getting my license back. In Illinois, getting driving relief means going through a formal hearing with the Secretary of State. There is a mountain of paperwork, strict requirements, and a hearing where you have to answer detailed questions. It’s overwhelming to handle on your own, which is why I hired Terri McAdams—and I am so thankful that I did. Terri made sure every piece of paperwork was accurate and complete. She walked me through my evaluation and all of my documents so I would be fully prepared. During the hearing, I misunderstood one of the questions and gave an answer that didn’t match my documents. Terri immediately recognized it was an honest mistake and stepped in with follow-up questions to clarify the situation for the hearing officer. Because of her quick thinking and her understanding of the process, my petition was granted. I can’t express how grateful I am for Terri’s help in getting my driving privileges back. She is knowledgeable, patient, and truly cares about her clients. If you need help getting your license reinstated, Terri is absolutely the person you want fighting for you.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Jason Haltom’s review raises broader issues that arise frequently in domestic battery cases. Many people are wrongly accused of these offenses, and I have successfully defended numerous clients at trial when the evidence showed they were innocent. Those cases are extremely important to me, and I work very hard to ensure that innocent people are not wrongfully convicted.
However, every case ultimately turns on the evidence presented in court. In Jason Haltom’s case, the court heard evidence that included a 911 call, body camera footage, and testimony presented at trial. Judges make decisions based on the evidence placed before them in the courtroom.
An attorney’s ability to defend a case effectively also depends on receiving accurate and complete information from the client so that the defense can be prepared appropriately for trial. That is true in every criminal case.
Because this matter has been raised publicly by Jason Haltom and because the proceedings are matters of public record, I will simply note that the outcome of the case was based on the evidence presented to the court. I stand by the work and preparation that went into the representation.

reviews.text: The first time I contacted them I was able to talk to Andrew directly that same day and tell him about my situation and ask questions etc. It was a nice long convo and he said if I had any questions to reach out to him. I told him I’ll have a response as to if I’m going to hire them by the end of the weekend. I then had some questions I texted him and didn’t get a response on. Multiple texts. Then I tried to call the office and him many times. They said they left him a message. They transferred me to him directly and still nothing. I did this at like 5 times over the course of a week and never heard back from him again. I was disappointed as I’ve heard good things and thought he’d be a great fit for my case but when there’s little to no communication in the hiring phase, imagine after being hired? Questions, concerns..wouldn’t be answered. Very disappointed I bad to go with someone else simply because a communication issue. I know lawyers are busy and have many clients but seriously? At least 5 times over a week and multiple voicemails left and text messages and nothing???

I regret my experience with this law office, as I felt they did not have my best interests at heart. Initially, I believed they were advocating for me, but I now feel misled and deeply disappointed-particularly by attorney Hope Nickel.
During a critical point in my case, I was struggling with anxiety, a condition I made known to my attorney.
Rather than supporting me through it, I felt that my mental health was used against me. Ms. Nickel told me, "You probably don't want to go to trial because of your anxiety," which influenced my decision to take a plea deal. I trusted her guidance, believing she was on my side, but I now feel that trust was misplaced. I regret not going to trial, a decision I made under pressure and without fully understanding the long-term consequences.
What makes this even more disheartening is that l was led to believe the plea would not remain on my record. That turned out not to be the case, and by the time I found out, it was too late. I also experienced poor communication and scheduling issues—| was once given the wrong court date and made a long trip unnecessarily, which showed a lack of organization and consideration.
Overall, I did not feel seen or heard throughout this process. I felt like my mental health was treated as a liability rather than something that required compassion and
understanding. I would not recommend this office, especially to individuals dealing with mental health challenges. I strongly encourage others to seek representation that truly listens and advocates for their well-being.
If you are ever in a pickle! Pick up that phone and call Andrew Nickel.

I was first charged with driving without insurance. While I was insured, I couldn't find my proof of insurance at the time of being pulled over. I was assigned a court date that I missed by mistake - thus, an ex parte judgement was made for me finding me guilty of charges that would then lead to fines and revoking of my license. At this time, I sought a consultation from Mr. Nickel's office and was really appreciative of his transparency in sharing that I may be able to reschedule my court date and appear with my proof of insurance without his assistance while still providing me with his rates should I choose to hire him. I chose to appear in court following his guidance and without representation.

My charges were dropped but when the county failed to follow up with updating the ILSOS, I was unaware that I was driving with suspended plates and license until I was pulled over months later. This resulted in additional charges of driving without registration and driving with a suspended license (which are Class A misdemeanors). I sought Mr. Nickel's representation through this process and appreciate how responsive and prompt he was to resolve my situation and help for my case to be dismissed. I did not have to appear to my second court date because he was able to resolve my case ahead of time.

Good Afternoon Everyone:

Every day, let us thank God for all his love and all his blessings, for all the trials and the people he puts in our path to overcome them. Therefore, today, February 10, 2025, I wish to express my most sincere gratitude to the law firm of OMAR SALGUERO – DUARTE & ASSOCIATES, P.C., for the excellent work they did on my asylum case. From the very beginning, I received professional, humane, and empathetic treatment from Dr. Omar Salguero and his team.
I also want to express my immense gratitude to attorney MARIA ROMAN for her dedication and commitment to the arduous task of gathering and submitting all the necessary documentation, as well as her guidance in the final court proceedings. Along with Dr. VICTORIA CARMONA, whose vast experience, leadership, and impeccable work in my final court proceedings were instrumental in the preparation and success of my process, which culminated in the approval of my asylum application. Thanks to all of them, who at every stage gave me confidence and support, resolving my doubts and guiding me clearly through each step.
I can say with certainty that the Asylum Process is a long road, one of many sacrifices and, above all, of very difficult moments. But with the hand of my God, He will bless and guide you as He did with me. He has allowed me to get to know this great Law Group and its team, which I can highly recommend to anyone who needs legal representation in immigration matters. I am deeply grateful for your support and for helping me achieve a new beginning in this country.

God bless you and allow you to continue helping and supporting all of us who seek your help and guidance.

A hug…

Dairon.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dairon, thank you so much for sharing these meaningful and grateful words! It was an honor for us to be with you every step of the way and to be able to provide the support you needed on such an important journey.

We recognize the effort and courage involved in an asylum case, and we are deeply happy to have been able to contribute to this new beginning in your life. We will continue working with the same commitment, empathy, and dedication to help more families like yours who are seeking a better future in this country.

May God bless you always, and thank you for recommending us!

I was the buyer in a real estate transaction where Aaron Vanagaitis represented the seller. Unfortunately, my experience was extremely stressful and unprofessional.

The seller disconnected power through ComEd before the final walkthrough, leaving the house without full power during the agreed closing time. Despite initially agreeing to hold the seller’s proceeds in escrow so an electrician could verify there was no damage, Aaron abruptly reversed that position the next morning and refused to cooperate further. His tone in communications felt hostile, condescending, and at times threatening, which made an already difficult situation worse.

We were ready to close and wanted the home — we even did close — but the way this was handled left us feeling harassed, unsupported, and mentally exhausted. We had young children with us and expected professionalism, not ultimatums.

This review is based entirely on my direct experience.

(Update in response to owner’s reply):

I want to clarify that my review is based entirely on our direct experience during the closing. The issue was not just a breaker being turned off — the power was fully disconnected via ComEd, leaving the home non-functional at the agreed walk-through time. This created real uncertainty and stress for us as buyers.

Despite this, we still closed because we wanted the home — not because we were trying to gain anything. What added to the frustration was the tone of communication we received afterward, which felt unnecessarily hostile and dismissive.

I shared this review to help future buyers know what to expect and to encourage more professional and respectful handling of such situations. I stand by what I wrote as an honest, fact-based account.
reviews.ownerResponse:
As you stated sir, we represented the seller. Yes they accidentally shut the power off. The power was on 1 hour after the closing had started, and your attorney refused to close. Even though she stated that you would close on the original closing date when we offered to hold back 45k for you to verify the power worked.
By doing this you breached the contract as a 1 hour delay in a closing is very reasonable.
You then demanded $1,500 from my client for a 1 day delay in closing that YOU caused by refusing to close. When we told you our client was not willing to reimburse for YOUR breach, you sent a cancelation. Which my client signed and was fully willing to cancel. Thankfully cooler heads prevailed and my client decided not to cancel and keep your EM for your breach.

We again offered late Thursday night to withhold all proceeds if you closed on Friday. But the power was fully on and working as the client was told every light in the house was on Thursday night, a neighbor visited and shut them off. At that point, there was no reason to withhold and proceeds, because everything was clearly working. We retracted the offer to withhold proceeds because your attorney never replied and accepted the offer, AND you could very easily do a new walkthrough prior to closing to verify everything was working. Your attorney got very upset with this because she believed that damage could occur by shutting off the main breaker to the house...... I fully explained my background in home construction and how this was not possible.
There was no unprofessional, simply talk of litigation, I apologize your attorney gave you bad advice and reassured you that you were not in breach of the contract, but you were. My client was already looking to litigate on Thursday evening when we explained what occurred.
Law isnt a place for feelings and emotions, and your attorney should have set proper closing day expectations that you should have made the entire day available for closing because issues do arise.

We wish you luck in your new house but slamming a business because you could not get additional money out of young family, is a new level of low.

The fact you believe Comed shutting off the power to a house would cause damage solely goes to show that you were never provided any proper expectations from your agents, nor your Attorney.
Sadly this is yet another scam account. After checking your account and previous reviews it's obvious You have never been a client at our firm. Expect a Cease and Desist order.

I don't think I've ever been treated so poorly by someone who presumably wants my business. There are A LOT of family/estate law firms in the area and I suggest you give your business to any other firm besides this firm.

Settling the estate of a loved one after they die can be complicated and confusing, especially when you and your family are mourning the loss, so it makes a lot of sense to contact an experienced attorney who can help you navigate the process. O'Flaherty Law offers a free 15 minute phone consultation, so I scheduled it hoping I could get some answers about the process. I followed all the instructions and waited for my call. Nearly 30 minutes after my appointment time I finally got a call from someone explaining that the attorney had to go to another meeting and they offered to re-schedule 3 days later. The re-scheduled appointment time came and went without a call or an email or anything. There must be a lot of dying going on and all the lawyers are so overwhelmed with business they cant bother themselves to keep an appointment that is really designed to generate more business.
